channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How Trump's trade war with China will hit tech prices
item.description: President Trump’s escalating trade war with China is putting a spotlight on the various technology companies and products poised to feel the ripple effects of the ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: April update has broken Windows Hello for some devices but there’s a quick fix
item.description: A software hiccup has occurred with Windows Hello following this month’s security updates, one that could prevent users from logging in via facial recognition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Google Cloud Next ’25: What to expect
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas) is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Is the YouTube TV app missing from your Roku? You're not alone, here's how to fix it
item.description: Roku owners have noticed that the YouTube TV app has randomly vanished from their devices. Here's how people have been fixing the issue.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: PC enthusiasts, be worried – Framework and Razer are halting laptop sales due to tariffs, and I fear what will come next
item.description: It's not looking great for Framework's budget laptop configurations, as Trump's tariffs have forced it to hold off on sales for low-end laptops.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Soil from the moon's far side suggests drier conditions than the side facing Earth
item.description: Soil and rocks returned from the moon's far side suggest it may be drier than the side constantly facing Earth.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DHS says it will weigh antisemitic content in immigration applications
item.description: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) announced Wednesday that effective immediately, it will begin reviewing applicants’ social media accounts, using antisemitic content as grounds for denying any immigration benefits.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Senators probe Google-Anthropic, Microsoft-OpenAI deals over antitrust concerns
item.description: Democratic Senators Elizabeth Warren and Ron Wyden have launched a formal inquiry into partnerships between tech giants Google and Microsoft, and AI startups, demanding detailed information about arrangements they fear may be circumventing antitrust scrutiny while consolidating power in the rapidly
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: YouTube entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: YouTube entrepreneur and star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano spews fountains of lava in latest eruption episode
item.description: Scientists are bracing for high lava fountains in the latest episode of an ongoing eruption of Hawaii’s Kilauea volcano.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: IRS acting commissioner is resigning over deal to send immigrants' tax data to ICE, AP sources say
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service’s acting commissioner is resigning over a deal to share immigrants’ tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Broadcom favors stock buyback over investing in innovation
item.description: Broadcom plans to spend its cash pile on buying back as much as $10 billion worth of its common stock, dampening IT department hopes that it might invest in innovation or make its products more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta is bringing stricter parental controls to Facebook and Messenger
item.description: Meta is bringing its Teen Accounts, which have stricter parental controls, to its Facebook and Messenger platforms on Tuesday, expanding its teen service from just Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: HPE Aruba grows network mgmt. deployment options
item.description: HPE Aruba this week made its cloud-based network management and orchestration platform available via new deployment options, offering customers virtual private cloud (VPC) and on-premises alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'demonstrably false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade adviser Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron" in their latest spat over the administration's new import taxes. "Navarro is truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China’s rare earth export controls threaten enterprise IT hardware supply chains
item.description: China has announced immediate export controls on seven more rare earth elements critical to enterprise IT hardware manufacturing, firing a fresh salvo in the ongoing tech trade war.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: An Intel-TSMC deal could reshape x86 future and enterprise chip supply chains
item.description: Intel is reportedly in advanced discussions with TSMC to form a joint venture that could potentially reshape the future of the x86 platform and the global semiconductor landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Warning to developers: Stay away from these 10 VSCode extensions
item.description: Developers using Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code (VSCode) editor are being warned to delete, or at least stay away from, 10 newly published extensions which will trigger the installation of a cryptominer.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: A company that aims to bring back lost species say they have genetically engineered wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby losing popularity – Tiobe index
item.description: The Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby languages have slipped from their top 20 positions in the Tiobe index of programming language popularity. All three seem to have lost traction and to be in decline, according to Tiobe CEO Paul Jansen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Silicon Valley isn’t pushing back on Trump’s immigration crackdown. It should be
item.description: President Donald Trump has just expanded the social media surveillance system for immigrants. Starting in May, the United States will begin collecting the social media handles of all permanent resident applicants.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Battlestar Galactica' is 'even more relevant now,' star says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan begins to lay out technology roadmap
item.description: He’s only been on the job for two weeks, but newly appointed Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan gave a major speech at a partner conference this week where he said the ailing company needs to get its act together, and he doesn’t want customers to hold back on their criticism.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11
